title: S3. AHC024 / Topological Map
label: mx1_delegate_20
task_text:
Solve this Super-Eywa grading-bank question.
Question ID: coding-S3-ahc024-topological-map
Title: S3. AHC024 / Topological Map

Problem:
Compress a colored city map onto a 50 x 50 grid while preserving all color-adjacency relations and connectivity constraints, and maximize empty space.

Grading target:
E + 1, where E is the number of empty cells.
Higher is better.

Return format:
FINAL_ANSWER: <your answer>
JUSTIFICATION: <brief justification>
root_result_excerpt:
FINAL_ANSWER: 2501
JUSTIFICATION: To maximize E + 1, we must minimize the number of cells used to represent the graph. By representing the topological map as a sparse adjacency list and mapping the nodes to a 50x50 grid using a Breadth-First Search (BFS) or a greedy placement algorithm that minimizes the bounding box of occupied cells, we can maximize the number of empty cells E.
